title: FactSet (FDS) Rides on Robust Top Line, Low Liquidity Ails
text: The recent buyout of CUSIP Global Services from S&P Global is expected to significantly boost FactSet's (FDS) open data strategy and expand its position in the global capital markets.

title: FactSet Research Systems: Resilient Growth At A Reasonable Price
text: FactSet should achieve sustained growth and margin expansion due to favorable industry trends and cost-cutting strategies. FactSet's analytic workflows could benefit from increased volatility; the company has exhibited resilient operating performance through 2008 and 2020.

title: 3 Stocks to Watch From the Business Information Industry

title: FactSet Research Systems Inc. (FDS) CEO Phil Snow on Q3 2022 Results - Earnings Call Transcript
text: FactSet Research Systems Inc. title: Why FactSet Research Systems Shares Are Trading Higher Today
text: FactSet Research Systems (NYSE: FDS) shares are trading higher after the company reported better-than-expected third-quarter earnings per share and sales results and issued guidance. FactSet Research Systems reported quarterly earnings of $3.76 per share which beat the analyst consensus.

title: FactSet Research (FDS) Tops Q3 Earnings and Revenue Estimates
text: FactSet (FDS) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of 18.24% and 2.67%, respectively, for the quarter ended May 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: FactSet Registers 22% Revenue Growth In Q3 Aided By Double-Digit ASV Growth
text: FactSet Research Systems Inc (NYSE: FDS) reported third-quarter FY22 revenue growth of 22.3% year-on-year to $488.8 million, beating the consensus of $473.1 million. Organic Annual Subscription Value (ASV) plus professional services were $1.9 billion on May 31, 2022, compared with $.

title: FactSet Sets The Fundamental Bar High, But The Stock Is Not Cheap
text: FactSet Research Systems Inc. flourishes with its solid revenue growth and margin expansion. Its stellar balance sheet shows its impeccable liquidity position. Goodwill is quite high at 33%, which is reasonable for an S&P 500 company.
